context.p: Miniconda is a minimal installer for Conda. It provides Python and the Conda package manager without the hundreds of extra packages that come with the full Anaconda distribution.

step.cmd: Go to the Miniconda download page and download the installer for your operating system (Windows or macOS).

info.note: On Windows, select Add Miniconda3 to my PATH environment variable when this option appears. This allows you to use conda commands in the VS Code terminal.

step.cmd: Accept all other default options and complete the installation.
Close and reopen VS Code to apply the PATH changes.
In the VS Code terminal, verify the installation by typing:
step.stepresult: The terminal prints the installed Conda version number, for example conda 23.5.0.

result.p: Miniconda and Conda are installed. You can now create virtual environments and install packages.
